reasoning: The threshold for a YES resolution requires Guinea to record more than 20 "Strategic developments" in a 30-day window (more than 10 times the historical baseline of ~1 event per month / 13 events per year). ACLED codes "Strategic developments" for events like high-profile arrests, peaceful territory transfers, kidnappings, or headquarters establishment, but NOT for standard protests or riots. Even during politically volatile periods such as the 2021 coup or widespread protests, Guinea did not typically record 20+ strategic developments in a single month. Absent an active and complex civil war triggering massive numbers of daily non-violent tactical events, hitting >20 in 30 days is extremely improbable. While upcoming 2025 elections could lead to instability or protests, those would predominantly be coded under other categories (Protests, Riots, or Violence against civilians). Therefore, the probabilities are set at or near the absolute minimum allowed (0.05), increasing slightly for farther-out dates to reflect the general increase in uncertainty over longer time horizons.
